in_scope: Create a new top-level `npm/` package for `@heurema/pactum` with a dependency-free Node ESM launcher, package metadata, checksum manifest, and hermetic node:test coverage.

Implement the launcher as a thin `npm/bin/pactum.mjs` entrypoint backed by testable pure helpers, including platform mapping, platform rejection, cache path resolution, checksum lookup, download verification, atomic cache install, and child process execution.
Add npm install documentation in `docs/install-npm.md` and link to it from the existing install documentation.
out_of_scope: Do not change `.github/workflows/release.yml` or add npm publishing, checksum generation, or bare-binary upload release automation.
Do not publish to npm, perform live GitHub Release downloads, or add any validation that depends on a published release asset.
Do not add optionalDependencies, platform-specific npm packages, postinstall scripts, Homebrew, Docker, Windows support, musl/Alpine support, signed provenance, or Go source changes.
acceptance: `npm/package.json` declares `@heurema/pactum`, `type: module`, `bin.pactum: bin/pactum.mjs`, placeholder version `0.0.0`, `files` limited to `bin` and `checksums.json`, Node engine `>=18`, repository/homepage metadata for `github.com/heurema/pactum`, and no runtime dependencies or install scripts.

`npm/checksums.json` is valid JSON and defines checksum entries for `pactum-darwin-arm64`, `pactum-darwin-amd64`, `pactum-linux-amd64`, and `pactum-linux-arm64`; missing or empty checksum values cause a loud nonzero failure rather than skipped verification.

The launcher maps only darwin/linux arm64/x64 to bare binary asset names using Go arch names, rejects Windows, musl/Alpine Linux, and unsupported platform/arch combinations before any network or cache work, and reports expected failures as single-line stderr messages without stack traces.

The launcher reads its own package version, caches verified binaries under `PACTUM_NPM_CACHE` or the XDG/home cache fallback at `<base>/<version>/<assetName>`, reuses an existing verified cached binary without redownloading, and uses the pinned URL `https://github.com/heurema/pactum/releases/download/v<version>/<assetName>` on cache miss.

Download logic follows redirects only to GitHub's release asset CDN host pattern, streams to a temporary file, verifies sha256 against the baked manifest before execution, deletes mismatched temp files, chmods successful binaries executable, and atomically renames into the versioned cache path.

The launcher execs the cached binary with `spawnSync(binaryPath, process.argv.slice(2), { stdio: 'inherit' })` and exits with the child status, or with a clear nonzero launcher error when spawn fails.

`npm/bin/pactum.test.mjs` uses `node:test` hermetically with no network or real downloads and covers supported platform mapping, x64-to-amd64 translation, Windows rejection, simulated musl/Alpine rejection, unsupported arch rejection, sha256 success/failure, version-scoped cache paths with `PACTUM_NPM_CACHE`, and missing/empty checksum rejection.

`docs/install-npm.md` documents global npm install, npx usage, supported OS/arch matrix, unsupported Windows and Alpine/musl behavior, cache location and `PACTUM_NPM_CACHE`, and the GitHub Release tarball as the manual alternative; existing install docs link to this page without claiming release automation is implemented in this slice.
assumptions: Node 18 or newer is available for local validation.
The follow-up release slice will overwrite the placeholder version and checksum values and publish the bare binary assets expected by this launcher.
Because JSON does not allow comments, any release-overwrite note for `checksums.json` must remain JSON-valid, for example via documentation or a JSON string field accepted by the implementation.
